title: 8 Strategy Concepts Every PM Should Know (10 minute read)
content: Strong product strategies combine several sources of competitive advantage. Those advantages must be chosen carefully and revisited as the market changes.

title: Is "jobs" a four-letter word? (5 minute read)
content: AI is changing Silicon Valley's definition of ambition from building large teams to needing fewer people. This shift could make hiring look inefficient and layoffs look impressive.

title: Your AI Should Keep a Diary (6 minute read)
content: An AI activity log makes invisible work easier to review and improve. It helps users delegate more effectively, preserve context, and build stronger AI habits.

title: Who's Afraid of Chinese Models? (11 minute read)
content: Chinese models are less of an economic threat than restrictive US AI policies. America should expand access to powerful models and support open innovation.

title: What Mr. Jambo and Levi's Can Teach Us About Listening to Customers (3 minute read)
content: Leaders often reshape customer feedback to confirm existing beliefs. Teams should test their assumptions and consider that customers may mean exactly what they say.

title: The Denominator That Matters (3 minute read)
content: Successful AI adoption requires technical understanding, problem knowledge, evolving practices, and trust. Without a credible social contract, resistance is rational.

title: The Power Of “Why?” (3 minute read)
content: Requirements often prescribe solutions instead of identifying real needs. Asking “why” and testing small increments helps teams preserve options and find simpler answers.

title: This Is How To Live A Good Life (7 minute read)
content: A good life is built through repeated action, not insight alone. We become better by practicing virtue, pursuing meaningful goals, and investing in relationships.

title: AI's Next Winners Won't Just Be the Frontier Labs (2 minute read)
content: AI's value is spreading beyond frontier labs.

title: The Rude Duck: What Are You Avoiding? (1 minute read)
content: Fear often hides behind reasonable excuses.

title: Is AI Going to Take PM Jobs? (7 minute read)
content: AI will reshape product management, not eliminate it. A broader technology downturn poses a greater threat to PM jobs than automation.

title: The Prototype Is a Question, Not a Product (8 minute read)
content: AI makes convincing prototypes easier to build, but polish can create a false sense of product readiness. A clear decision framework helps teams define what each prototype can prove, gather the right evidence, and prevent experimental code from becoming an accidental production commitment.

title: Are PMs becoming irrelevant? (5 minute read)
content: AI is replacing routine PM work, not strategic product judgment. The PMs who thrive will focus on choosing the right problems and creating measurable value.

title: The Farabi's Guide to Building Products That Matter (3 minute read)
content: Great products help users flourish rather than merely capture attention. Shared purpose, careful research, and continuous iteration keep teams focused on that goal.

title: How to Create a Truly Inspiring Product Vision (9 minute read)
content: A strong product vision captures the lasting positive change a product aims to create, rather than describing features or business goals. Concise, emotionally engaging, and collaboratively developed, it motivates teams and provides stable direction for product strategy.

title: All Things Product Podcast with Teresa Torres & Petra Wille (17 minute video)
content: Customer data can reveal what to investigate, but rarely what to build. Rich, story-based interviews provide the context teams need to make better product decisions.

title: Reactors Have a Surprising Amount of Detail (6 minute read)
content: Early momentum can hide the details that determine whether a product succeeds. Rigorous testing and continuous iteration turn promising technology into something reliable.

title: Code Yellow, Code Red (8 minute read)
content: Code Yellows help teams resolve serious engineering problems before they become emergencies. Clear ownership, measurable exit criteria, and transparent communication keep the response focused and temporary.

title: AI products generate more signal than teams know how to use (2 minute read)
content: AI conversations reveal what users want and where products fall short.

title: Generated and suppressed demand (2 minute read)
content: Improving teams often uncover hidden demand, causing their backlogs to grow again.

title: The Reverse Information Paradox (3 minute read)
content: Companies risk surrendering proprietary knowledge as their employees use and improve external AI systems. To retain that value, enterprises must control their data, feedback, evaluations, memory, and learning infrastructure while remaining independent from any single model provider.

title: The Most Human Technology Ever Made (5 minute read)
content: AI's greatest promise is not simply saving time. It gives more people the ability to create, experiment, and turn their ideas into reality, making individuality more valuable than technical expertise, capital, or permission.

title: Taste cannot be delegated (7 minute read)
content: AI can generate endless design options, but it cannot determine which one best serves a product's ambition and context. Strong design still requires a responsible leader who can apply taste, resolve tradeoffs, and confidently choose a direction.

title: Five Years Building the Context Layer - and Why It Belongs in the Agentic Loop (24 minute read)
content: Graphlit built a powerful production platform but failed to become a scalable business because it lacked repeatable distribution and a clear, outcome-driven market position. Its core insight remains: agents need a portable context layer that stays separate from the runtime while supporting every stage of the work with durable organizational knowledge.

title: AI's Biggest Winners Have the Lowest Margins (6 minute read)
content: Low-margin businesses could become some of AI's biggest winners because modest cost reductions can dramatically increase profits. The greatest opportunity lies in embedding AI into existing operations to remove coordination work without requiring employees to change how they work.

title: How to Collect Product Feedback When Your AI Gives Every User a Different Answer (3 minute read)
content: AI outputs vary from one interaction to another, so traditional feedback methods often provide an incomplete picture. Product teams should focus on users' next actions, review a representative range of real outputs, and capture the context behind poor results.

title: The Forgotten Measure of Data Quality: Decision Quality (3 minute read)
content: Clean, reliable data has little economic value unless it leads to better decisions. Data teams should define the decisions each product is meant to improve and measure that impact alongside traditional quality metrics.

title: Incubate, Compound, Refinance, Liquidate (4 minute read)
content: AI's value depends on whether it moves software assets toward healthier economics, not simply whether it makes teams faster.

title: With AI Agents, Attention is the New Bottleneck (2 minute read)
content: AI makes execution cheap, so the advantage shifts to teams that manage attention and judgment well.

title: Meta admits its first ‘superintelligence' was too stupid to survive for three days (2 minute read)
content: Meta pulled Muse Image after its lack of clear consent controls sparked immediate privacy and misuse concerns.
